tool.name: Phase E rbest overlap
tool.description: Gene orthology edges via reciprocal-best chain block projection
tool.command: mkdir -p chains_dir annotations_dir && ## Stage the rbest-chain list collection. Element identifier is "{a}.{b}"; ## the script globs *.rbest.chain and recovers the strain pair from the stem. #for $c in $chains ln -s '$c' 'chains_dir/${c.element_identifier}.rbest.chain' && #end for ## Stage the gene-BED list collection. Element identifier is the strain name; ## the script globs *.bed and uses the stem as the strain name. #for $b in $annotations ln -s '$b' 'annotations_dir/${b.element_identifier}.bed' && #end for python '$__tool_directory__/phase_e_rbest_overlap.py' --chains 'chains_dir/*.rbest.chain' --annotations 'annotations_dir/*.bed' --strains '$strains' --min_overlap $min_overlap --output '$rbest_edges'

param.name: chains
param.label: Reciprocal-best chain list collection
param.help: A 'list' collection of UCSC chain files, one per strain pair. The element identifier must be the pair name '{a}.{b}' (strain_a.strain_b); it is restaged as '{a}.{b}.rbest.chain' so the strain pair can be recovered from the file name.

param.name: annotations
param.label: Gene BED list collection
param.help: A 'list' collection of gene BED files, one per strain. The element identifier must be the strain name (matching the '{a}.{b}' pair names); it is restaged as '{strain}.bed'. Column 4 is the gene id.

param.label: Strains
param.help: Comma-separated list of strain names (e.g. strainA,strainB). Recorded for provenance; edges are derived from the chain pair names and BED element identifiers.

validator.message: Letters, digits, underscore, hyphen, dot and comma only

param.label: Minimum reciprocal overlap fraction
param.help: An edge is emitted only when the target gene is covered by aligned chain blocks at least this fraction (overlap_a) AND its best query gene reciprocally overlaps the projection at least this fraction (overlap_b). Default 0.90.

tool.help:
**Phase E rbest overlap** — gene orthology edges via reciprocal-best chain block projection.
For each reciprocal-best (rbest) chain between a pair of strains, every **target**-strain gene interval is projected through the chain's *aligned blocks* into **query** coordinates, then connected to the single best query gene that overlaps the projection. An edge is emitted only when:
- the target gene is well covered by aligned blocks (`overlap_a >= min_overlap`), **and** - its best query gene reciprocally overlaps the projection (`overlap_b >= min_overlap`).
Block-level projection (rather than the whole-chromosome header span) avoids the cartesian explosion of spurious edges.
Ported from `Pv4-pangenome/v3/pipeline/impl/scripts/phase_e_rbest_overlap.py`.
-----
**Inputs**
- *Reciprocal-best chain list collection* — a `list` collection of UCSC chain files, one per strain pair. The element identifier is the pair name `{a}.{b}`; it is restaged to `{a}.{b}.rbest.chain` so the strain pair is recovered from the file name. - *Gene BED list collection* — a `list` collection of gene BED files, one per strain. The element identifier is the strain name; it is restaged to `{strain}.bed`. Column 4 is the gene id. - *Strains* — comma-separated strain names (provenance only). - *Minimum reciprocal overlap fraction* — `--min_overlap` (default 0.90).
**Output**
- `rbest_edges.tsv` — tab-separated edges with columns `strain_a`, `gene_a`, `strain_b`, `gene_b`, `overlap_a`, `overlap_b`. When no edge passes the threshold the file still carries the header row.
-----
**Implementation note**
The wrapper iterates each input list collection and symlinks every element into a staging directory under the file name the bundled script globs for (`{a}.{b}.rbest.chain`, `{strain}.bed`), then passes the globs to the script. The helper (`phase_e_rbest_overlap.py`, stdlib only) walks the chain blocks with a forward-strand projection and a bisect-indexed query gene lookup.
